name: Enseada de Sagres (anchorage)
approachNotes: Protected from N and NW swell by Ponta de Sagres headland; open to S and SE. Huge sandy expanse; preferred by cruisers over Baleeira for swinging room. Anchor in 5-8 m on sand/mud. Katabatic gusts possible in afternoons from surrounding cliffs. Enseada de Baleeira (37 00.7N, 8 55.4W) 0.7 nm E offers easier shore access and some fishing-boat moorings; both bays are fair-weather only - strong S or SE swell makes both untenable.
nearestTown: Sagres village (~1.5 km); fuel station up the hill; limited provisioning; no crew-change airport nearby (Faro ~110 km by road)
notes: First stopping point after rounding Cabo de Sao Vicente (3.5 nm W). Classic passage anchorage for waiting for favourable conditions or wind shift before heading E. Lighthouse beam sweeps anchorage. No marina facilities; land by dinghy on beach.
orcaNotes: Cabo de Sao Vicente / SW Iberia is a hotspot for orca interactions with yachts (GTOA). Follow current advisories; keep close inshore / shallow per CCS guidance when rounding.
verifyNeeded: exact depth soundings in both bays

name: Porto de Baleeira (Sagres fishing harbour)
approachNotes: Harbour entrance at ~37 00.7N, 8 55.4W (confirm on chart). Small commercial fishing harbour with seawall offering shelter. Free anchorage to W of seawall end or among fishing boats. Can be gusty in afternoons. No designated visitor berths; used as passage stop only. Open to S.
swellSensitivity: moderate - seawall provides partial shelter but open to S
nearestTown: Sagres (~1.2 km walk); gas station in town; daily fish auction at harbour; basic shops
notes: Operational fishing harbour managed by Docapesca. Used as overnight stop before or after rounding Cabo de Sao Vicente. Access to Sagres Fortress and town. Anchorage is primary option; berthing against wall occasionally possible subject to fishing-vessel movements.
orcaNotes: Adjacent to Cabo de Sao Vicente orca-interaction hotspot. Check GTOA/CCS advisories before rounding.
verifyNeeded: depth inside harbour for visiting yachts

name: Marina de Lagos
marinaHours: Jun 1-Sep 15: 08:00-21:00; shoulder: 09:00-19:00; winter: 09:00-18:00. Lifting bridge on same schedule (call VHF 09).
approachNotes: Approach on bearing 282 between west harbour mole head and tower of Santo Antonio church. Follow channel to waiting pontoon to starboard, immediately before the lifting bridge. Request bridge opening on VHF 09 - not available during 10-min window before daily train departures. Basin dredged to -2.80 m; channel to minimum 3.0 m. Fuel pontoon (diesel + unleaded 95) on starboard near bridge.
nearestTown: Lagos old town (<200 m walk); excellent provisioning, restaurants, chandlers; Faro airport ~80 km; Lagos train station adjacent
notes: One of the 'gateway' marinas to the Algarve; designated customs clearance port. Full-service boatyard (Sopromar) adjacent in fishing harbour. Blue Flag and Euromarina Anchor Award. Lifting-bridge restriction is the key operational factor. Tidal access all states.
orcaNotes: Offshore SW Portugal route has had orca interactions; risk decreases east of Lagos.

name: Alvor - anchorage / tidal lagoon
approachNotes: CAUTION: shifting bar - ALWAYS verify current depths before entry. Entrance between two training walls at approx 37 07.08N, 08 37.14W; walls have green/white and red/white banded light structures. At shallowest point bar can have as little as 0.5 m at LAT - sandbanks constantly shift. Entry for drafts <=2.0 m only, on rising tide from mid-tide. Inside pool: max ~4 m but silting common. Red buoy marks shallowest section.
swellSensitivity: low inside lagoon; moderate-high at entrance in onshore swell
bestTideEntry: Rising tide from mid-tide; never at LW or on a falling tide
nearestTown: Alvor village (~500 m); fish restaurants, limited provisioning; Portimao 5 km for full provisioning
notes: Charming working fishing harbour/lagoon, 2.5 nm E of Lagos. Excellent holding in sandy mud. Very popular at weekends. Not recommended for keelboats >=2.0 m draft. Sounding essential on approach - treat published depths as indicative only.
verifyNeeded: bar depth - must be sounded or verified with port authority before entry

name: Marina de Portimao
approachNotes: Enter from due S between two long moles (~250 m wide entrance). Leading line: align Ferragudo fore and aft beacons, then course 020 - leave buoy No.2 to port, continue to buoy No.4, then bear to marina entrance. Bar entrance depth 8.5 m; quay depth 4 m. Deep-water access at all tides. Reception pontoon to starboard. Full boatyard (Ferragudo, E bank) with 50 t and 300 t travel lifts.
nearestTown: Portimao centre (~500 m); supermarkets, provisioning; Faro airport ~65 km. Good crew-change hub.
notes: Designated customs clearance port. Deepest draft access in the Algarve (5 m). TYHA 5 Gold Anchors. Superyacht capable. 14 nm E of Albufeira.

name: Marina de Albufeira
approachNotes: Approach from E, entering outer basin between two breakwaters on bearing ~315. Light structures: red/white (port) and green/white (starboard) on breakwater ends. Channel dredged to 4.0 m. Inner basin from outer basin; finger pontoons 2.5-4.0 m. Described as the most sheltered marina in Portugal.
swellSensitivity: low - enclosed outer and inner basins
nearestTown: Albufeira centre (~20 min walk via bridge); tourist hub, good provisioning; Faro airport ~40 km.
notes: 15 nm E of Portimao, 7 nm W of Vilamoura. Boatyard: 70 t travel lift, 6 t crane, repair booths. TYHA 5 Gold Anchors, Blue Flag. Book ahead in summer.

name: Marina de Vilamoura
approachNotes: Call 'Vilamoura Radio' VHF 09 (24h) or Ch 16. Marina control tower beacon 18 m (range 19 nm). E entry beacon green/white, W entry beacon red/white (5 nm). W mole 660 m, E mole 430 m. Entry depth 4.0 m; inner basin 3.3 m and 2.0 m. Vessels >35 m LOA / >3 m draft call ahead. Reception quay and fuel station in inner channel.
nearestTown: Vilamoura resort (on doorstep); Faro airport ~27 km. Excellent crew-change hub - closest large marina to Faro airport.
notes: Largest marina in Portugal; designated customs/immigration clearance port (SEF). 'Best Marina in Portugal' repeatedly. Very busy Jul-Aug; quiet Oct-May. Vilamoura Nova Marina (superyacht, 2024) is a separate adjacent facility.

name: Ria Formosa - Barra do Farol entrance
approachNotes: TIDAL GATE: currents reach 4-7 kt at springs through the Faro-Olhao inlet. Enter on flood only; do NOT depart on strong ebb with onshore wind - severe overfalls at entrance. Leading transit: green/white beacon on eastern mole aligned with Santa Maria lighthouse. HAZARD: large shallow area off SW entrance point - do not cut the corner from/to the W. Main channel depth 6-12 m. Once inside, turn right at first green buoy for Culatra, or NW for Faro/Olhao channels.
swellSensitivity: low inside lagoon; entrance dangerous in strong onshore wind on ebb
bestTideEntry: Flood tide; ~1-2 h after LW; avoid spring ebb
nearestTown: Farol village on Ilha da Culatra (no vehicles); ferry to Olhao
notes: Main deep-draft entrance to Ria Formosa (Barra de Faro-Olhao), constructed 1952. Only entrance suitable for yachts >1.5 m draft. Magnificent natural lagoon inside.
orcaNotes: Some orca reports close inshore W of entrance; maintain depth until clear of offshore shoals.
verifyNeeded: entrance waypoints vs current chart (Admiralty 89 / PT 23403)

name: Ilha da Culatra - anchorage (Ria Formosa)
approachNotes: Turn right at buoy No.6 inside the Ria Formosa entrance. Depth 5-6 m, excellent holding in sandy mud. Two areas: (1) Farol village - abeam lighthouse, small (2-3 boats), out of main stream; (2) Culatra village - large popular anchorage (~40 vessels) but thick weed beneath sand causes drag - check holding. Fast water taxis; chop builds in strong W against tide.
swellSensitivity: very low - protected by barrier islands; choppy in strong W
bestTideEntry: Via Barra do Farol on flood (see entrance entry)
nearestTown: Culatra village (dinghy ashore): cafe, ferry to Olhao ~20 min for full provisioning
notes: Most celebrated anchorage in the Algarve; car-free island; hundreds of boats in summer; excellent base for exploring Ria Formosa by dinghy.

name: Porto de Recreio de Faro (Doca de Recreio)
approachNotes: Via Ria Formosa inner channels from Barra do Farol. Follow channel NW to buoy 23, then anchor or moor in Doca Recreio; dinghy to far wall. Strong currents 3-5 kt at springs. Long fetch in channel when windy at HW. Primarily naval/yacht-club facility. A larger Porto de Recreio de Faro (~277 vessels) was in planning as of 2024.
bestTideEntry: Rising tide; arrive near slack before flood
nearestTown: Faro old town (<500 m); excellent provisioning; Faro International Airport 5 km. Best crew-change point in region.
notes: Anchor or moor to explore Faro city and use the airport. Mostly club berths - visitor space limited; new larger marina project may change this.

name: Porto de Recreio de Olhao (Marina de Olhao)
approachNotes: Access via Ria Formosa inner channels after Barra do Farol. Follow buoyed Olhao channel NW. Navigable 365 days. Depth at MLW 2-3 m alongside. MAX LOA 17 m is a critical limitation. Significant tidal flow. Travel lift for haulout.
bestTideEntry: Via Barra do Farol on flood
nearestTown: Olhao centre (~200 m); outstanding fish market, provisioning; Faro airport 15 km. Very good crew-change.
notes: Inside Ria Formosa Natural Park. 17 m max length significantly restricts larger yachts - confirm fit before planning. Mechanical workshop on site.

name: Tavira - anchorage / Ria de Tavira
approachNotes: Entrance moles approx 37 06.707N, 7 36.928W (Barra de Tavira); approach ~275 until close to W breakwater, then ~325 through entrance - deeper water to port. CAUTION: strong tidal currents 3-4 kt; fishing nets in approach. Inside, follow channel to port, anchor ~3 m SW of jetties. Lay bow AND stern anchors against tidal stream. Ria de Tavira shallow lagoon - max draft 2.0 m; rising tide only.
swellSensitivity: low - protected by Ilha de Tavira; chop in channel
nearestTown: Tavira old town (~500 m); charming Moorish architecture; good provisioning; Faro airport ~37 km.
notes: No marina - anchorage only. One of the prettiest towns in the Algarve. Tight space; may share mooring field. ~3 m spring range -> powerful current. Daylight arrival only.

name: Marina de Vila Real de Santo Antonio (Porto de Recreio do Guadiana)
approachNotes: Rio Guadiana entrance ~12 nm E of Tavira; 600 m mole on W bank marks entrance; channel buoyed ~1 nm offshore. Bar waypoints (2 m draft at LW neaps): 37 09.686N 7 23.829W; 37 09.268N 7 23.641W; 37 08.724N 7 23.693W. STRONG cross-current at entrance - dangerous other than near slack. Ebb 2-4 kt, flood 1-2 kt. Marina ~2 nm upriver on W bank, S of ferry dock. Accessible HW +/- 3 h.
swellSensitivity: low inside river; entrance rough with onshore wind on ebb
bestTideEntry: Slack water at entrance; approach HW +/- 3 h
nearestTown: Vila Real de Santo Antonio (adjacent, <200 m); good provisioning; ferry to Ayamonte (Spain); Faro airport ~55 km.
notes: Easternmost marina in Portugal, on the Guadiana marking the Spain/Portugal border. Ayamonte (Spain) directly across (5-min ferry). River navigable ~20 nm upstream. Strong tidal currents dominate berthing. EU customs formalities possible here.
